key_insight: A non-overlapping Schwarz hybrid FE-neural operator method couples FE and Point-DeepONet solvers through Neumann-Dirichlet exchange, removing overlap overhead while handling irregular solid mechanics domains.
engineering_relevance: The framework can accelerate high-fidelity simulation of nonlinear or dynamic solids by preserving FE accuracy where needed and using learned operators in expensive subdomains.
method: Non-overlapping Schwarz alternating coupling with traction transfer from NO to FE
Point-DeepONet on unstructured FE point clouds for irregular geometries
Analytical strain/stress derivation from displacement operators to enforce mechanical consistency
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on three benchmarks—static linear elasticity, quasi-static hyperelasticity, and elastodynamics—across both regular and irregular geometries, with reduced inner Schwarz iterations and bounded error accumulation over long horizons.
watch_item: Performance depends on stable interface coupling and careful training of the NO subdomain; traction-based exchange may be sensitive to noise or poor generalization near strongly nonlinear interfaces.
paper_title: A Non-Overlapping Schwarz Hybrid Finite Element-Neural Operator Framework for Solid Mechanics on Irregular Domains
